result.summary: domains.googleapis.com exposes the Google Cloud Domains HTTP API plus the `gcloud domains` CLI surface; docs indicate OAuth 2.0 access tokens are supported, and the v1beta1 discovery document also exposes API-key query auth.
result.description: Cloud Domains is a Google Cloud service for registering and managing internet domain names within Google Cloud projects. It lets users configure and manage domain registrations and related settings programmatically or through Google Cloud tools.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Create or choose a Google Cloud project in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), enable the Cloud Domains API as described in [Roles and permissions](https://docs.cloud.google.com/domains/docs/access-control), then authenticate with Google Cloud. The easiest path for developers is to install the [Google Cloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) and run `gcloud auth login` or `gcloud auth application-default login` to obtain OAuth 2.0 access for your account/application; service-account based auth can also be used through Google Cloud IAM.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: In the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials), open **APIs & Services** → **Credentials**, create an API key, and restrict it to the Cloud Domains API/project as needed. The Cloud Domains discovery document for `v1beta1` says the `key` query parameter is accepted unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.
